title: Purely Smoky Vietnamese Pork Bun Cha
description: A smoky and juicy Vietnamese dish featuring slow-cooked pork and a hint of icing sugar, served with a Mediterranean-friendly twist in a bun cha form.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together fish sauce, lime juice, icing sugar, garlic, and ginger to make the marinade.
Add the sliced pork to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and cook the marinated pork for 5-7 minutes per side, or until charred and cooked through.
Cook the rice noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
Assemble the bun cha by placing a portion of noodles on the bottom of a bowl, followed by a few slices of the grilled pork, some pickled carrots, and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.
Garnish with additional cilantro and pickled carrots if desired.
Offer a side of the marinade as a dipping sauce for the pork and noodles.
